data_source_summary: Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26 Demand for Grants No. 6; Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26; Official Notification No. 6-BD/2010/CGN (updated 2023); State Forest Portal

Q1.src: Official Notification No. 6-BD/2010/CGN; Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26 Demand for Grants No. 6

Q2.v: Forest Department, Chhattisgarh; co-implementing agencies: Industries Department, SFFC (State Forest Federation Corporation)
Q2.src: Official Notification No. 6-BD/2010/CGN (2023 update) — 'Implementation: Forest Department + Industries Department + SFFC'

Q3.v: Official Notification No. 6-BD/2010/CGN (January 1, 2010; updated 2023); aligned with National Bamboo Mission; Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26 Demand for Grants No. 6; Outcome Budget 2025-26

Q4.r: Bamboo is cultivated on state forest land (18,500 hectares) formally notified for community use under Notification No. 6-BD/2010/CGN. The scheme designates state forest land for plantation, production, and artisan/farmer community benefit. Land ownership is with the State Forest Department and resources are designated for non-exclusive community use.
Q4.src: Official Notification No. 6-BD/2010/CGN; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 — area verified by land survey

Q5.v: Bamboo forest resource on state land in Chhattisgarh. Area: 18,500 hectares (cumulative, CAG-verified by land survey). Resource type: bamboo (shoots, culms, processed material) as a forest produce. Geographic spread: distributed across state forest zones. Production scale: 4.85 lakh metric tons annually. Boundaries documented via plantation mapper on state forest portal and CAG land survey.
Q5.src: CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; State Forest Portal plantation mapper; Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25

Q6.r: State forest portal has a plantation mapper covering the 18,500-hectare area. CAG (2024-25) verified area via land survey. Official Notification No. 6-BD/2010/CGN specifies plantation target of 18,500 hectares as a publicly documented boundary.
Q6.src: State Forest Portal — plantation mapper; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25

Q7.r: Bamboo as Minor Forest Produce (MFP) carries statutory rights under the Forest Rights Act 2006 (Section 3(1)(c) — community rights over MFP including bamboo) and PESA 1996 for scheduled areas. Notification No. 6-BD/2010/CGN provides explicit legal framework for artisan and farmer community access to bamboo resources, making the community's right legally enforceable.
Q7.src: Forest Rights Act 2006 Section 3(1)(c); PESA 1996; Official Notification No. 6-BD/2010/CGN

Q8.r: CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 reviewed the scheme and confirmed operational status with no diversion findings. Scheme documents show no active mining lease, SEZ notification, land-use change order, or compulsory acquisition affecting the 18,500-hectare bamboo plantation area.
Q8.src: CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; Official Notification No. 6-BD/2010/CGN (2023 update)

Q9.breakdown: Bamboo plantation ₹30cr; Processing infrastructure ₹25cr; Artisan training ₹15cr; Market linkage ₹5cr. Actual disbursement: ₹72cr (96% utilisation).
Q9.src: Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26 Demand for Grants No. 6; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25

Q10.r: All four components flow to community/commons benefits: (1) Bamboo plantation ₹30cr — on state forest land (community forest commons). (2) Processing infrastructure ₹25cr — community processing centres managed through SFFC (State Forest Federation Corporation), serving the collective artisan community. (3) Artisan training ₹15cr — intangible knowledge/skill commons (training 42,000 artisans in community craft). (4) Market linkage ₹5cr — community-level market infrastructure. No component flows exclusively to a private actor; all outputs are community-accessible through the SFFC framework.
Q10.src: Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26 Demand for Grants No. 6; Official Notification No. 6-BD/2010/CGN

Q11.r: No expenditure in the scheme flows exclusively to private assets. Processing units (285) are registered under SFFC/state framework and serve the collective artisan community. Individual artisan income support (₹3,500/ha farmer payments) is an output transfer, not a capital expenditure item. Q10 + Q11 = 75 + 0 = 75 = Q9.
Q11.src: Official Notification No. 6-BD/2010/CGN; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25

Q12.interp: PASS — θ₁ = 1.00 ≥ 0.50 threshold. 100% of scheme expenditure flows to community/commons benefits.
Q12.src: Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26 Demand for Grants No. 6; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25

CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 — ₹72cr expenditure verified, 96% utilisation
Official Notification No. 6-BD/2010/CGN (updated 2023) — budget mandate and component structure
Outcome Budget 2025-26 — expenditure results and targets
Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25 — scheme financial and output data

Q14.r: Benefits are partially excludable: directly accessible to registered artisans (85,000), farmers in bamboo zones (18,500 ha), and supply chain workers (28,500); non-enrolled individuals are excluded. However, the enrolled group is large and openly accessible to forest-area communities. Indirect ecological benefits (watershed, forest cover) are non-excludable to all. Classified as 1 (partially excludable) conservatively; the scheme could be argued as 2 given open registration.
Q14.src: Official Notification No. 6-BD/2010/CGN; Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25
